checks.finding: REQ-001 (requirements.md:46-58) specifies exact deletion mechanism, scope (task stage only), LF/CRLF handling, and sh/ps1 byte-for-byte and decision-for-decision parity, directly measurable via AC-001..AC-004.

The single Goals entry REQ-001 is covered by AC-001 through AC-005 (requirements.md:82-114), spanning masking effect, negative controls, CRLF/no-trailing-newline, sh/ps1 parity, and delivery-boundary evidence; no orphan goal.
Each AC (requirements.md:82-114) states an observable outcome (exit code, literal output string, byte-identical hash, identical diagnostic rule ID, or SHA-256 equality) rather than vague intent.
Non-goals (requirements.md:60-69) explicitly exclude check-task-state content validation, hook-guard changes, other-stage normalization/registry/identity-ledger changes, and retroactive re-hashing.

Security Boundaries (requirements.md:162-170) and Roles and Permissions (requirements.md:124-132) state the integrity/authorization constraints; the round-2 edit added AC-005/TEST-005 and the R-10 hook-guard citation as the delivery-boundary enforcement mechanism, closing the round-1 approval-boundary gap per the precheck edit_summary.
Risks (requirements.md:185-197) lists three high-risk claims each mapped to a named mitigation/validation surface (AC-002; AC-003/AC-004; AC-005 plus staged-copy TDD and suite re-run).
